title: Diplodoc (redirects.yaml)

vcs.description: Enable VCS-based redirect tracking (detects renames from Git history).
vcs.markdownDescription:
**`boolean | VcsConfig`** Enable VCS-based redirect generation.

- `true` — track file renames using the full Git commit history
- Object — enable with a specific `initialCommit` as the history start point

```yaml
vcs:
  initialCommit: abc1234
```

initialCommit.description: Commit SHA used as the start of the Git history scan for renames.
initialCommit.markdownDescription: **`string`** Git commit SHA to use as the starting point when scanning history for file renames. Commits older than this are ignored.

from.description: Old URL path to redirect from (relative, without leading slash).
from.markdownDescription: **`string`** Old URL path to redirect **from** (e.g. `old/page.html`). Relative to the docs root, without a leading `/`.

to.description: New URL path to redirect to (relative, without leading slash).
to.markdownDescription: **`string`** New URL path to redirect **to** (e.g. `new/page.html`). Relative to the docs root, without a leading `/`.

Redirect.description: A single redirect rule mapping an old URL path to a new one.
Redirect.markdownDescription:
**`Redirect`** A single URL redirect rule.

Both `from` and `to` are paths relative to the documentation root (without leading `/`).

markdownDescription:
Diplodoc redirects file (`redirects.yaml`).
Defines URL redirects that are applied during the `publish` step. Each redirect maps a set of old paths to a new path.
```yaml # Simple redirect old/path.md:
  - from: old/path.html
    to: new/path.html

# VCS-based redirects (track file renames via Git history) vcs: true ```
